Visualisation benefits

1. Engagement - a well crafted story maintains audience's attention
2. Improved understanding - it makes complex processes and technical structure more accessible
3. Effective communication - visualisation simplify data by highlighting key insights into a coherent sequence.

![Decorative image: Different types of visualisation.](data:image/png;base64,)

Steps

1. Find the story in the data. Identify findings that will direct your story.
2. Consider your audience
3. Determine the data that matters
4. Analyse and question your insights. How might others interpret your insights. What question might they have?

Steps

1. Introduction and context
2. Data visualisation
3. Key insights
4. Connection of dots
5. Actionable insights

Key steps for informative design

1. Show causality
2. Establish credibility
3. Use multivariate data
4. Show comparisons
5. Integrate annotations
6. Focus on your message

Principles for graphical integrity

1. Proportional numerical qualities.
2. Labelling to reduce ambiguity. Explanation of the data on the graph as well.
3. Data variation, not design variation. If multiple charts are on the same slide make sure they are separated
4. Avoid nominal or unadjusted money. Deflated and standardised units of monetary is better than nominal units.
5. Number of variables = number of dimensions. Number of information carry variable(dimensions) should not exceed the number of dimensions in the data.
6. Avoid unintended context. How could typical viewer misinterpret it

[Data Science Principles: Topic 2: Data preparation and analysis](onenote:#Data%20Science%20Principles%20Topic%202%20Data%20preparation%20and%20analysis&section-id={DA506E3A-CAEC-426F-863D-D8E5205525B5}&page-id={45E7201F-32C2-442C-82C7-10AC38F79A79}&end&base-path=https://d.docs.live.net/37cecb0168167465/Documents/Personal%20\(Web\)/Learning/L7%20AI.one)

### Pythons visualisation tools

Matplotlib Lot of functionality and serves as foundation for other visualisation libraries.

Seaborn built on top of matplotlib, a higher-level interface, simplifies the creation of complex visualisation such as heatmaps. Works well with Pandas data structures.

Matplot lib,

Figure - canvas

Add axes - plots

One or more artists -glyphs

![](data:image/png;base64,)

API mode is OO method.

## Geopandas

GeoDataFrame - A pandas dataframe with a geometry column

GeoSeries - A series object designed to store shapely geometry objects, like points, line and polygons

Shapely is a package for geometric objects.

Four main operations

1. Reproject data
2. Spatial Join - Joins to GeoDataFrames based on spatial relationship
3. Buffering
4. Calculating area and length

Reproject adding layer details

![A diagram of two maps – the left-hand map is a silhouette of the UK, whereas the right-hand map has an overlay of detail showing the boundaries of counties in the UK.](data:image/png;base64,)

Spatial Join - Joins to GeoDataFrames based on spatial relationship

![Diagram: Spatial join as described in the preceding text.](data:image/png;base64,)

Buffering

![Diagram: A map of Europe, in which countries in close proximity to the UK are highlighted.](data:image/png;base64,)

Other visualisation libraries

- Altair
- Bokeh used to make interactive visualisation for web browsers
- Geoplotlib - maps and plotting
- Ggplot -
- Plotly
- Pygal - SVG(Scalar Vector Graphics)

## Deployment

- Standalone apps on local machines
- Internal servers
- Cloud

Factors that impact deployment

- How frequently will your ML solution run
- Who will be the users
- Is it for internal staff only
- Is it for known customers or public facing
- Is it an internal component of a larger system
- What data does it need for input
- What data will it output
- What sort of traffic is expected
- What latency is acceptable

Cloud or lambdas could scale.

![Diagram of interaction with endpoints as described in the preceding text.](data:image/png;base64,)

![A diagram showing communication between clients and server with HTTP, as described in the preceding text.](data:image/png;base64,)

# Deploying a ML model using Flask
